name: Roll-Driven Flipbook (Word Cycle → Optional Lock)
one_line: A hi-hat roll drives a centred word that flips every 16th-note through a word list; optionally the flicker resolves and locks into a final phrase.

match.notes: The ONE signal that triggers this template is a fast sustained-fill rolls[] entry in the audiomap (hundreds of hits/min, ~16th-note). A roll = a dense grid of slots with no single readable message → fill it with a rapidly-cycling word flipbook. Contrast held-message (onset desert → freeze) and typewriter-phrase (continuous onsets → one word per onset): here the RATE is roll-level (≈4× faster than onsets), so words flicker, they don't read one-at-a-time. The lock-to-phrase resolve is OPTIONAL.

word_flicker.notes: PROPOSED motion verb. A centred word that hard-swaps (0ms) every 16th-note, cycling the word list to FILL the roll window. Word count auto-derives from (rollEnd-rollStart)/16th. The typographic field IS the percussion.

lock_phrase.notes: PROPOSED motion verb. OPTIONAL. On the SURGE after the roll, the flicker clears and a scrambled line decodes left→right, locking the final phrase on the next downbeat. Empty resolveText → skip; the last flicker word just holds (locks) instead.

background.notes: Pure black in this reference impl (per the reverse brief). The original gruop_3 layered a rotating 3D shape + glitch field here — an OPTIONAL add-on role, not required by the structural type.

flipWords.note: comma-separated; cycled to fill the roll

beat_map.comment: Script-side BEAT block in index.html. SCENE-RELATIVE (starts at ~0, no dead pre-roll) — the source's absolute roll at 3.71s is rebased to flipStart. Duration is single-sourced from data-duration; the flipbook AUTO-FILLS the flicker window.

source_roll_absolute.note: rebased: subtract ~3.6s offset to remove the pre-roll
